# [After Market Car Dzxiaso Radio Install on 2007 Lexus ES 350](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xZt56EEIwLA)

**Visibility**: Public
**Uploaded by**: [Bsanch17](https://www.youtube.com/@bsanch17)
**Uploaded at**: 2023-09-01
**Published at**: 
**Length**: 30:00
**Views**: 35166
**Likes**: 444
**Category**: Autos & Vehicles

## Description

```
Installing a after market, tesla style, Dzxiaso radio on a Lexus ES 350. this works for 2007 - 2012 year models.
#lexus #radio #car #es350 #diy 

Dzxiaso for LEX ES350 Radio Upgrade 2007-2012,Android Stereo Replacement,IPS Touch Screen,Quad-core CPU,2G RAM 32G ROM,Build in Wireless carplay,Android Auto

Product Links
Radio
https://a.co/d/iLx3LWX
T-Taps
https://a.co/d/7puHDhg

Chapters:
00:00 - Intro
00:37 - Radio Unit and Connections
06:11 - Removing Old Radio Unit
14:18 - Installing New Radio Unit
17:55 - Installing Back Up Camera
23:12 - Mounting Radio and Re-assembly
26:56 - Routing Cables and Mounting BackUp Camera
29:01 - DONE! Everything Works!
```

![Liked](https://www.clublexus.com/forums/images/badges/other/likes/most-likes-desktop-large.png)

![Default](https://www.clublexus.com/forums/images/icons/icon1.gif)**Replacing Stereo on 2011 without Nav**

* * *

I've been researching stereo replacement options on my wife's 2011 ES without nav. I'm upgrading for wireless CarPlay, a backup camera and improved sound. I've spent a lot of time on-line, including with Crutchfield. I went to a couple of shops too, but I still have a few questions I could use advice on. I'm looking at a top-of-the-line mech-less Kenwood, double-din, with a 6.9" LCD and as an alternative, the top-of-the-line Kenwood with a 10.1" floating screen. BTW...I was happy to find an inexpensive Metra dash bezel and bracket kit for prepping the hole for a double-din.

Here's my questions:

1\. Has anyone installed a floating screen in a non-nav 5th-gen ES? If so, what do you think of it? My biggest concern is with it being a target for thieves.

2\. When shopping for a wiring interface kit to hook up to the factory amp, the Axxess TYTO-01 description says it is for interfacing with the OEM JBL amp and speakers. As far as I know, the "eight speaker, non-nav system" is not JBL, so I'm hesitant about the application. I watched a YouTube vid of a guy removing an amp in a 2013 ES and it was clearly a Pioneer. Does anyone know if the 2011 non-nav stereo system is JBL? Crutchfield says it fits my vehicle, but the description of the product also says it fits 2001 to 2010 Toyotas. Any words of wisdom (or experience)?

I've done a similar upgrade in 2001 Tundra, and the Crutchfield harness I got simply by-passed the OEM amp. But the ES, eight-speaker system will likely need to retain the amp, so the interface is critical. I'm very happy with the sound quality in the Tundra, and hope to get a comparable result in the ES.

[![Bigtrain's Avatar](data:image/svg+xml;utf8,<svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 50 50'><circle class='avatar-circle-default' fill='%23a73864' cx='25px' cy='25px' r='20px'></circle><text class='avatar-text-default' x='49%' y='53%' fill='white' text-anchor='middle' alignment-baseline='middle'>B</text></svg>)](https://www.clublexus.com/forums/members/395665-bigtrain.html)

**3rd Gear**

Joined: **Jul 2022**

Likes: **2**

From: **PA**

![Default](https://www.clublexus.com/forums/images/icons/icon1.gif)

* * *

I just put in a Kenwood into my 2012 Non-NAV ES 350. Because the AC controls are above the stereo, I would worry about covering those with a large floating screen. I can try to post a pic of my system. Works well with the items ordered from Crutchfield, but ordering with the "Ready Harness" service would have saved me hours of wiring and hassle. I wired my backup camera to have power all of the time and still trigger with reverse. Speed wire does not need to be hooked up. I'm having a little trouble locating a good wire route to place the GPS antenna for Carplay and it does not work well just sticking it on top of the stereo under the dash as some may suggest. Hope this helps. Items I ordered are shown below. Amp and speakers sound good.

**Lexus ES330 07-12 MS**

12040LX11 Toyota & Lexus antenna adapter

707V17IRTJ Boyo VTL17IRTJ

794ADSMSW iDatalink Maestro ADS-MSW

113DDX9907 Kenwood Excelon DDX9907XR

120998162G Metra 99-8162G Lexus Kit

120TYTO01 Axxess TYTO-01

I just put in a Kenwood into my 2012 Non-NAV ES 350. Because the AC controls are above the stereo, I would worry about covering those with a large floating screen. I can try to post a pic of my system. Works well with the items ordered from Crutchfield, but ordering with the "Ready Harness" service would have saved me hours of wiring and hassle. I wired my backup camera to have power all of the time and still trigger with reverse. Speed wire does not need to be hooked up. I'm having a little trouble locating a good wire route to place the GPS antenna for Carplay and it does not work well just sticking it on top of the stereo under the dash as some may suggest. Hope this helps. Items I ordered are shown below. Amp and speakers sound good.

Thanks Bigtrain...this is exactly the type of info I was looking for! Nice job on your installation!

I definitely want the "Ready Harness." Regarding the speed sensor wire, from my experience, unless Kenwood reprogrammed their top units in the last two years, **wireless** CarPlay doesn't work without connecting the speed wire. I've heard the GPS antenna can be placed under the dash, if, you can find a place with no metal between the antenna and the sky (glass and plastic aren't an issue). In my Tundra, I placed the antenna on top of the dash, but it is not sticking due to the high heat from the sun.

# [**5**](https://www.clublexus.com/forums/es-1st-to-4th-gen-1990-2006/988397-replacing-stereo-on-2011-without-nav.html#post11333594 "permalink")

[![Bigtrain's Avatar](data:image/svg+xml;utf8,<svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 50 50'><circle class='avatar-circle-default' fill='%23a73864' cx='25px' cy='25px' r='20px'></circle><text class='avatar-text-default' x='49%' y='53%' fill='white' text-anchor='middle' alignment-baseline='middle'>B</text></svg>)](https://www.clublexus.com/forums/members/395665-bigtrain.html)

[Bigtrain](https://www.clublexus.com/forums/members/395665-bigtrain.html)

**3rd Gear**

Joined: **Jul 2022**

Posts: **3**

Likes: **2**

From: **PA**

![Default](https://www.clublexus.com/forums/images/icons/icon1.gif)

* * *

Wilson2000,

I heard that too about the speed sensor wire and wireless Carplay with iPhone, but I also saw that there may be a fix in firmware updates. My head unit was up to date out of the box and I did not connect the speed sensor wire and wireless Carplay works fine. I've never even used wired Carplay. The issue that I'm having with Carplay is more on the phone side. I have an older 6S and it heats up and locks up when using GPS and streaming music for any length of time. Probably time for a new phone. I did order a cooling fan for my phone to see how that works.

The "alternate" wiring for the parking brake simplifies things a little too and works just fine.

I'll have to keep working on the GPS. I think I'm going to fish mine out the center dashboard speaker grill and mount it on top of the dash near the air vent at the windshield.

This was a fairly intense project on a car that was new to me. I'm not great at finding wires in the dash and so I just ran the reverse signal wire from the tail light area. That may sound crazy, but it was easier to me since I already had to run wires for the camera and found a good route into the trunk next to the back seat trim.

The harnessing between all of the modules required for this install would have been taken care of with Ready Harness, but I forgot to order it. I did IM with Crutchfield before the order and they apparently forgot to mention it to me as a good thing to order. I called Crutchfield support and they whipped up a PDF of wire to wire connections on the spot while I was on the phone with them. Crutchfield is the best for service. The Metra adapter plate was defective twice, but they got me replacements fairly quickly. The first had a malformed screw boss for one of the AC control mounts and the second attempt had messed up gray finish.

One thing to note too is that the steering controls work with the Maestro module, but this is all the Maestro does for you on this year ES 350 without NAV. You don't get to see any of the cool "extra" data or control AC from the head unit. The car just isn't integrated to that level apparently. There are a ton of adjustability and EQ adjustments for the speakers and amp. I will probably replace the stock speakers next year, but they really are very good for old stock speakers.

We love our new ES 350 even though it's 10 years old already. It's our first Lexus and my wife now doesn't want anything else. I traded in my 2010 Prius for it, which I loved driving for all of the 173,000 miles. The V6 power of the ES 350 is great. It's like graduating to a big boy car from a toy.

Good luck on your project!

Also, the stock AMP is on the passenger side trunk wall way under the carpet and trim. I didn't think that I had one and was thinking that another tiny module may have been it for a while. I finally saw it, but didn't get a close enough look to see if it is a JBL or not. If I recall correctly, I thought that I saw somewhere that the NAV system has a JBL so I'm not sure if they have JBL in non-NAV too but just not as many speakers? It doesn't really matter to me since it seems to work just fine at this point and I don't plan to upgrade the amp.

[![Twdll's Avatar](data:image/svg+xml;utf8,<svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 50 50'><circle class='avatar-circle-default' fill='%2364c683' cx='25px' cy='25px' r='20px'></circle><text class='avatar-text-default' x='49%' y='53%' fill='white' text-anchor='middle' alignment-baseline='middle'>T</text></svg>)](https://www.clublexus.com/forums/members/286829-twdll.html)

Thread Starter

**Driver School Candidate**

Joined: **Sep 2016**

Likes: **0**

From: **TX**

![Default](https://www.clublexus.com/forums/images/icons/icon1.gif)**2011 ES350 Stereo Upgrade - keeping OEM head unit**

* * *

_**HOWDY.**_

I have just purchased a **2011 ES 350**(Non ML with Nav) and obviously, the stock system sucks, eh... I mean em... is inferior to the Mark Levinson upgrade. ![](https://www.clublexus.com/forums/images/smilies/smile.gif)

I am curious if anyone knew or would know the answers to these questions?

**1.** First, does anyone happen to know if it is possible to replace the stock Pioneer amplifier with a used ML amplifier?

Is it "plug and play? I read that Club Lexus user "lexusaudio" stated that it is indeed "plug and play", unplug one, and plug the other in, but I couldn't find much info on switching them, or if it **was even a good idea to do so, or just go another route**.

**2**. Secondly, I was curious about upgrading the speakers all around. Would it not be possible to purchase a couple of 8-ohm 6x9's (for the front doors, using adapters), a couple 8-ohm 3" tweets (dash) and a couple of 8-ohm 6.5 for the back doors (using adapters)?

Even if the amplifier is used to playing to 6-ohms speakers. (I think that the higher ohms means less stress on the amp, but also less power.)

I would not use an Infinite Baffle subwoofer in the back either. I think the best route is to use an aftermarket sub, with another amp.

Thanks.

* * *

_Last edited by Twdll; Sep 11, 2016 at 12:14 PM._

[![mlacomb's Avatar](data:image/svg+xml;utf8,<svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 50 50'><circle class='avatar-circle-default' fill='%23b6a6a9' cx='25px' cy='25px' r='20px'></circle><text class='avatar-text-default' x='49%' y='53%' fill='white' text-anchor='middle' alignment-baseline='middle'>m</text></svg>)](https://www.clublexus.com/forums/members/167052-mlacomb.html)

Joined: **Oct 2011**

![Default](https://www.clublexus.com/forums/images/icons/icon1.gif)

* * *

I have an 08 with non nav non ml. Honestly, for what it's worth - I added a JL sub + amp + AudioControl LC2i. Putting the stock post-amplifier speaker wiring into the sub amp itself resulted in a very low signal; the LC2i cleaned this right up

I would love to have some more ability to customize the sound (equalization - the mid-highs are almost screaming in this car), but for most things I listen to it does a much better job than any other non-customized car setup I've had. i have had a pair of component JL speakers sitting in my garage for >1 year, but haven't gotten around to tearing it apart yet... I figure since I usually sit up front, I was only going to invest in getting those ones done. The rear door panel speakers are really just to fill in (potentially not full range from what I've heard elsewhere around here).

# [**3**](https://www.clublexus.com/forums/es-1st-to-4th-gen-1990-2006/833903-2011-es350-stereo-upgrade-keeping-oem-head-unit.html#post9618152 "permalink")

[![Twdll's Avatar](data:image/svg+xml;utf8,<svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 50 50'><circle class='avatar-circle-default' fill='%2364c683' cx='25px' cy='25px' r='20px'></circle><text class='avatar-text-default' x='49%' y='53%' fill='white' text-anchor='middle' alignment-baseline='middle'>T</text></svg>)](https://www.clublexus.com/forums/members/286829-twdll.html)

[Twdll](https://www.clublexus.com/forums/members/286829-twdll.html)

Thread Starter

* * *

**Driver School Candidate**

Joined: **Sep 2016**

Posts: **3**

Likes: **0**

From: **TX**

![Default](https://www.clublexus.com/forums/images/icons/icon1.gif)

* * *

I've been doing some research alot on ClubLexus and a few other sites, and here's what Ive learned since I posted this thread. It seems the best route is NOT to get the ML amp (as I've seen countless threads about getting them repaired) and replace the stock speakers with aftermarket ones.

To do that (and someone correct me if I'm wrong), we would have to add a processor... something like the AudioControl LC7I or LCQI, **added Post Amp**. This would work the best _bang for your buck._

Then I could (in theory) hook up my two amps, and the rest of my aftermarket speakers (an entirely different discussion) still have all the normal functions in the cars climate, Nav, and Stereo.

I could also leave the wires connected to the center channel speaker connected to the stock amp and still hear the Nav Voice Guidance from it.

All of this is predicated on the two words... In theory.
